name: MPI Rank Binding Diagnostics
summary: Collect MPI rank placement and CPU binding evidence from Slurm jobs.
description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose MPI rank placement, CPU binding, cpus-per-task, and topology assumptions before comparing performance or scaling simulation workloads.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm account or allocation for the short diagnostic job.

inputs.name: partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue for the diagnostic job.

inputs.name: mpi_module
inputs.description: Optional MPI module to load before compiling and launching the probe.

inputs.name: binding_policy
inputs.description: Slurm srun --cpu-bind policy to test, such as verbose,cores or verbose,none.

inputs.name: task_shape
inputs.description: Nodes, tasks per node, cpus per task, and total task count for the small diagnostic run.

outputs.name: topology_report
outputs.description: Node and Slurm environment evidence, including optional lscpu, numactl, and hwloc output.

outputs.name: rank_affinity_report
outputs.description: TSV report mapping MPI ranks to hosts, processor names, visible CPU counts, and affinity masks.

outputs.name: launch_plan
outputs.description: Dry-run launch plan showing compile and srun command lines before execution.

examples.title: MPI rank binding diagnostic Slurm job
MPI rank and CPU affinity probe

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Submit the wrapper with RUN_MPI_BINDING_PROBE=0 to review the plan, then set RUN_MPI_BINDING_PROBE=1 on a short test partition and confirm rank-affinity.tsv is written.
